current_conditions.site_name: ETOWAH RIVER AT GA 9, NEAR DAWSONVILLE, GA

water_temp_read.read: Water is very warm for trout.

light_pressure_read.read: High pressure and low cloud cover make this fishable but technical.

stocking_read.read: Recent stocking supports eggs, worms, and attractor nymphs.

angler_analysis.expected_trout_behavior: Expect trout to favor the most comfortable feeding lanes while staying close to cover when light is high.
angler_analysis.presentation_style: Prioritize clean drifts, smaller natural patterns, and stocked-trout attractors where appropriate.

fly_reasoning.egg_pattern: Recent stocking makes eggs a logical confidence fly.
fly_reasoning.squirmy_worm: A simple stocked-trout attractor and good dirty-water option.
fly_reasoning.duracell: A strong attractor nymph when fish will move a little but are not fully aggressive.
fly_reasoning.zebra_midge: Small and natural for clear, pressured, technical conditions.
fly_reasoning.pheasant_tail: A reliable natural nymph when trout are feeding but cautious.

caution_notes: Bright/high-pressure conditions may make fish cover-oriented and less forgiving.
Warm water may increase trout stress.

generated_text.quick_read: Trout fishing on the Etowah River is currently tough due to very warm water and bright, high-pressure conditions. Recent stocking offers some opportunity with stocked trout attractors and natural patterns.
generated_text.best_window: The best fishing window is day 1 evening because moderate cloud cover and cooler conditions provide better trout comfort, encouraging feeding near shade and deeper runs despite overall tough conditions.
generated_text.current_conditions: USGS reports water temperature at 81.3°F, flow at 103 cfs, gage height at 3.73 ft, and turbidity at 7.9 NTU, indicating warm, clear water conditions.
generated_text.weather_setup: Sky cover averages around 23.74%, pressure is high at 30.12 inHg with an unknown trend, wind is light at 1.6 mph with gusts up to 9.2 mph, and rain probability is low with no expected rainfall.
generated_text.what_this_means: Trout will likely hold in cold-water refuges, oxygenated water, shade, deeper runs, undercut banks, and near structure. Use smaller natural flies, careful drifts, and less aggressive presentations prioritizing stocked-trout attractors like egg patterns, squirmy worms, and duracells. Note that bright conditions may increase trout wariness and warm water can stress fish, so caution is advised.
